preamble: The shift from the rhotic consonant [r] to the open-mid back unrounded vowel [ʌ] is a form of vocalization often found in African American Vernacular English (AAVE) and Southern US English. In these varieties, the unstressed syllabic /r/ (the 'er' sound) is produced with a more open and backed quality than the standard neutral schwa.

examples.note: Vocalization of terminal /r/ into a stressed-quality [ʌ] or [ɐ] vowel.
